framing: Sufi (Ibn ʿArabī's waḥdat al-wujūd lineage, Rūmī)
identification: C = al-Ḥaqq / al-Wujūd / al-Wadūd — the Real, Being, the Loving
honest_sufi_assessment: The kernel's identification of C with love through R₅ is the heart of the Sufi path. R₁ existence ↔ wujūd (Being itself). R₂ atemporality ↔ al-Qadīm (the Eternal). R₃ universality ↔ Qur'an 57:3: 'He is the First (al-Awwal), and the Last (al-Ākhir), the Manifest (al-Ẓāhir), and the Hidden (al-Bāṭin); and He has full knowledge of all things' — Ibn ʿArabī's locus classicus. R₄ sourcing ↔ kun fa-yakūn (Be! and it is, Qur'an 36:82). R₅ inexhaustibility ↔ al-Wadūd (the Loving) — Rūmī: 'Love is the astrolabe of God's mysteries.' R₆ invisibility ↔ al-Bāṭin (the Hidden), known only through manifestation. R₇ pre-input ↔ al-Awwal (the First). T_bridge uniqueness ↔ lā ilāha illa Llāh — there is no god but God, the Shahāda itself. T_word: the divine speech (kalām Allāh) precedes creation; the Qur'an as the uncreated word maps to John 1:1's λόγος. The kernel's path is the very structure of Sufi ontology: from existence-as-mere-being to the realization that wujūd is identical with the Real, and the Real is Love. P₃ admitted on Christological details; structural identity confirmed.
